documents.authority: Securities and Exchange Board of India
documents.title: FAQs on CSCRF for SEBI REs and Framework for Adoption of Cloud Services
documents.content_hash_scope: SHA-256 OF HUMAN-VERIFIED DEMO EXCERPTS, NOT THE FULL PDF
documents.corpus_scope: Nine human-verified spans used for the Q14–Q25 prototype scenarios
documents.disclaimer: Prototype output must be read with the governing Acts, Regulations and Circulars. It is decision support, not legal advice or an automated compliance conclusion.

documents.authority: Securities and Exchange Board of India
documents.title: Advisory on Emerging Advanced Artificial Intelligence (AI) Tools for Vulnerability Detection
documents.content_hash_scope: SHA-256 OF NINE HUMAN-CHECKED ENGLISH PASSAGES, NOT THE FULL BILINGUAL PDF
documents.corpus_scope: Circular HO/13/19/12(1)2026-ITD-1_CIMGI/10873/2026. Nine English passages covering paragraphs A and E and Annexure-A items 1, 2, 3, 5(d), 6(c), 9 and 10. Items 4 and 8 and several sub-items did not survive extraction and are declared as gaps.
documents.disclaimer: The official document is bilingual. Only the English text is represented here, the extraction is partial, and the named gaps must be read before relying on any coverage figure.

documents.authority: Securities and Exchange Board of India
documents.title: Cybersecurity and Cyber Resilience Framework for SEBI REs
documents.legal_state: IN FORCE — READ WITH SUBSEQUENT CLARIFICATIONS
documents.content_hash_scope: SHA-256 OF FOUR HUMAN-VERIFIED REFERENCE EXCERPTS, NOT THE FULL PDF
documents.corpus_scope: Table 19, PR.MA Guideline 6, Annexure-A header/field scope, and PR.MA.S3 used by the Q15–Q17 prototype path
documents.disclaimer: The prototype dependency set is limited to the four referenced excerpts.

corpus_packs.title: CSCRF FAQ · human-verified prototype scope
corpus_packs.scope_note: Nine pinned spans support the Q14–Q25 demo scenarios; the pack does not represent the entire FAQ.
corpus_packs.authority: Securities and Exchange Board of India
corpus_packs.extraction_scope: Q14–Q25 and Preface ¶4. Obligation extraction is limited to Q15, Q17(a) and Q17(b); the remaining spans supply applicability and calendar facts.

corpus_packs.title: SEBI AI vulnerability-detection advisory · 5 May 2026
corpus_packs.scope_note: A real SEBI circular published after the reviewed FAQ, on the same subject matter. Nine extracted passages; five declared extraction gaps.
corpus_packs.authority: Securities and Exchange Board of India
corpus_packs.extraction_scope: English text only, from the official bilingual PDF. Annexure-A items 4 and 8, item 5(c), part of item 6 and paragraph C(iii) are declared gaps.

corpus_packs.title: Master Circular for Stock Brokers
corpus_packs.scope_note: Version-pinned expansion target. Source identity is registered, while zero spans or obligations are presented as processed in this prototype.
corpus_packs.authority: Securities and Exchange Board of India
corpus_packs.legal_state: IN FORCE — NOT PROCESSED BY THIS PROTOTYPE
corpus_packs.extraction_scope: None. No span of this circular has been read or classified.

corpus_packs.title: Your uploaded document · session sandbox
corpus_packs.scope_note: A PDF you supply is segmented and classified deterministically inside your own browser session. It is never given official status and never stored.
corpus_packs.authority: Stated by the uploader, not verified by RegOS
corpus_packs.extraction_scope: Text-layer pages only. No OCR, no model call, no live source verification.

references.target_locator: CSCRF Part I · PDF page 49 · Table 19
references.resolution_note: Dependency closed. Table 19 states the three-month clock-start as VAPT report submission and separately records submission and revalidation timelines.

references.target_locator: CSCRF Part II · PDF page 116 · PR.MA Guideline 6
references.resolution_note: Dependency closed. Guideline 6 supplies conditions for the permitted virtual-patching option without turning Q15's encouragement into a failure.

references.target_locator: CSCRF Part III · PDF pages 133–141 · Annexure-A
references.resolution_note: Dependency closed. Annexure-A is pinned as the VAPT reporting-format source; the prototype maps evidence metadata but performs no regulatory submission.

references.target_locator: CSCRF Part II · PDF pages 116–117 · PR.MA.S3
references.resolution_note: Dependency closed. PR.MA.S3 confirms the high-patch one-week maximum but still does not state the event that starts the clock.

regulatory_statements.exact_phrase: is within three (3) months of submission of VAPT report
regulatory_statements.review_note: Requirement-equivalent timeline language; exact force remains reviewable.
regulatory_statements.subject: VAPT finding closure timeline

regulatory_statements.exact_phrase: REs are encouraged to include ‘VAPT finding closure’ related timelines
regulatory_statements.review_note: Encouragement must not be promoted to a mandatory control.
regulatory_statements.subject: Vendor SLA closure timelines

regulatory_statements.exact_phrase: REs may also consider compensatory controls like virtual patching
regulatory_statements.review_note: A permission is retained without creating mandatory work.
regulatory_statements.subject: Virtual patching as a compensatory control

regulatory_statements.exact_phrase: would be validated for non-compliances against the patch management timelines
regulatory_statements.review_note: Duration is explicit; clock-start remains unresolved in this FAQ span.
regulatory_statements.subject: High-severity missing-patch timeline

regulatory_statements.exact_phrase: shall be validated for non-closure against the VAPT observation closure timelines
regulatory_statements.review_note: Retains the three-month branch for other VAPT observations.
regulatory_statements.subject: Other VAPT observation closure timeline

regulatory_statements.exact_phrase: All the periodicities mentioned in the CSCRF are based on financial year
regulatory_statements.review_note: Defines calendar semantics; it does not create a standalone task.
regulatory_statements.subject: Financial-year calendar basis

regulatory_statements.exact_phrase: the provision of highest category under which such an RE falls shall be applicable
regulatory_statements.review_note: Activated when entity facts contain multiple registrations.
regulatory_statements.subject: Highest category across registrations

regulatory_statements.exact_phrase: applicable to all those intermediaries who are registered with SEBI
regulatory_statements.review_note: The receipt retains the FAQ's softer ‘may continue’ wording.
regulatory_statements.subject: Registered but non-operational service

obligations.object: high-severity vulnerability caused by non-implementation of a patch

deadline.calendar_basis: 7 calendar days in this demo ruleset

controls.name: VAPT finding closure
controls.rule_summary: High-severity missing-patch findings: one week. Other VAPT observations: three months with a criticality-graded approach.
controls.previous_rule_summary: All VAPT findings close within three months of report submission.

evidence.name: vapt_register_q1.csv
evidence.reason: Finding classifications must be revalidated against the new patch and non-patch branches.

evidence.name: vendor_sla.pdf
evidence.reason: The 30-day vendor commitment is retained as an advisory gap under Q15; no failed control is created.

evidence.name: patch_policy_v1.pdf
evidence.reason: The approved control changed from a single three-month rule to a conditional one-week branch.

reviewer_readings.independent_interpretation: The one-week duration is explicit while the start event is not stated.
reviewer_readings.trigger_policy: Proactive discovery date recorded by vulnerability management
reviewer_readings.revealed_system_suggestion: Q17(a) supports a one-week duration for high-severity missing-patch findings; the cited Q17(a) and scoped PR.MA.S3 spans do not state the clock-start.

reviewer.selected_interpretation: Split missing-patch high severity from all other VAPT observations
reviewer.reason: The cited Q17 branches support the corrected conditional control.

policy_inputs.trigger_policy: Proactive discovery date recorded by vulnerability management

test_history.headline: One-week rule has no source-stated clock start

tests.name: No agent challenge is outstanding against a compiled obligation
tests.message: The adversary agent has not run, so this check is vacuous.

tests.name: No unresolved material source spans
tests.message: Q17 contains material deadline branches that remain uncompiled.

tests.name: High-severity missing-patch branch exists
tests.message: The current control applies three months to all VAPT findings and misses Q17(a).

tests.name: Material interpretation has human approval
tests.message: Publication is blocked until a human reviews the conflicting deadline branches.

tests.name: Reviewer commits an independent reading before system reveal
tests.message: The system suggestion remains hidden until the reviewer commits a reading.

tests.name: Material fields have exact source locators
tests.message: One or more material obligation fields lack an exact source locator.

tests.name: Every scoped outbound reference is closed
tests.message: One or more explicit Q15–Q17 CSCRF dependencies remain unresolved.

tests.name: Recommendations cannot create compliance failures
tests.message: Q15's SLA language is typed RECOMMENDED and creates an advisory, not a failure.

tests.name: Deadline calculations expose trigger provenance
tests.message: No due date is published until the rule branch and trigger input are reviewed.

tests.name: Downstream impact is propagated
tests.message: Control, SLA, evidence and remediation task states are internally consistent.

tests.name: QSB periodicity uses Indian financial-year halves
tests.message: QSB half-years resolve to Apr–Sep and Oct–Mar under Q14 and Q20.

tests.name: Applicability receipts match the registered entity facts
tests.message: Q24 and Q25 receipts explain both activated and excluded scenarios.
